Partner(s): Matt O’., Anna Re., Jakob Ha.
Notes: Fun climb that's hidden in plain sight! Would be super down to lead it sometime

Partner(s): Matt O’., Anna Re., Jakob Ha.
Notes: TRd just the first pitch to the Roy Gap Chimney's anchors

Partner(s): Chris Co., Matt O’., Anna Re., Jakob Ha.
Notes: Always a delight to climb this puppy :)

Partner(s): Chris Co., Matt O’., Anna Re., Jakob Ha.
Notes: Super wet! Chris put it up for us to break into 8s at Seneca, super impressive since it was so damn soggy :o Kudos to him!

Up at 5:30, climbing by 8. I took the first four pitches linked into two 200 footers, then hung out in a heinous hanging belay for an hour and a half while Matt and Tom worked on a snafu with the haul bag. I did the first half of the next pitch, but came down for Matt to tackle the free section ahead. I got to do the challenging C2 part, though! Matt took the next block of two, then Tom the last three in the dark with my headlamp. Topped out just before midnight, I barely squeaked in my Duolingo with four minutes to spare! Rapped along the fixed lines after stashing supplies for Matt and Toms push on Triple-D. Walked back to Camp 4 and in bed by 4am. Saw so many tree frogs after dark on the rock, some mice, a softball sized fluffy rodent, and some mountain quail near the base! 22 hours camp to camp.
Final 5.6 scramble to summit. Descent was long and sketch!

Hitchhiked to the approach trail, jugged to the start of P3. I led P3-5, the aid cruxes, and Matt took us to Dano Ledge after dark on P6-8. What a trip!! Saw a fighter jet fly through the valley, got a sun shower in the evening that didn't touch us because of how leaning the leaning tower is. Made dinner around midnight. So worked!
Fixed P1&2, Matt led P2 then we rapped and spent the night in camp 4
First ledge poop ever! Went quite well. Got to do a wild lower-out from under the roof. Rapping down from the top went so fast!! Todd and Forrest are total Gs
Pre-fixed the rope on P5 while Matt and Tom were setting up the portaledge below the roof. Forgot the dang tagline so I had to back-aid it as well! What an awesome line!

Partner(s): Tom Cr., Matt O’., Anna Re., Zoey Sp.
Notes: Bit of a pump fest, crux right off the ground.
INSANE CHOSS FEST >:(
New line Matt put up, it was worthy! Was getting dripped on by a little water coming from a crack in the crux, but pulled through!
Always wanted to do this line just because I see it from the start of the via all the timeeee
Sick setting! Very worried about loose rock the whole time though
Such a sweet climb! If the crux had one more crimp I would have been outta there

Using this line to train for el cap. Led P1, jugged P2, followed P3 free in my approach shoes. Rowdy line, super fun on aid! Wild roof pull on P1 was quite interesting in ladders. Matt almost pissed on me while I was jugging P2!!
Basically if the gunks was limestone. So many horizontals!
Aid practice for El Cap. A creative lead!
Climbed smooth and confidently which saw me through. Excited for another lead attempt!
Went left at the crux and fell, then went right and it went! Took at a couple more bolts because I already biffed the onsight.
Followed Matt on P1 and led P2. Rowdy!! Climbed into the sunset and saw a bald eagle fly through Roy gap from the top of P2
Chill scramble day
Chill scramble day
Soloed to the last pitch then roped up for the crux pitch
Simuled with Matt on a booty run

Fell on a bomber .75 at the crux. Tried to lieback but should have just stayed in the crack and went for the foot jams. Followed Matt on the OG second pitch left of the roof, super licheny! Two more adventure pitches to get to the top of the southern pillar
Followed Matt. Thin and greasy, but good! Venom was sick
